title: The longer I trade, the more I realize psychology matters
author: Ok_Beginning_8126
selftext:
Over the past few years I realized most of my bad trades came from emotions, not lack of opportunities.   



Chasing momentum too late, forcing entries out of boredom, panic-selling during volatility… probably made every mistake possible at some point.   



Lately I’ve been trying to keep things simpler and focus more on process before every trading week. Mostly paying attention to trend strength, momentum, volume behavior, and whether a stock still looks extended after big runs.   



Some of the names I’ve personally been watching most closely recently are NVDA, GOOG, MSFT, META, TSLA and a few higher-volatility growth plays.   



Not saying any of these are guaranteed winners obviously. Just companies and sectors I still find interesting from a momentum and long-term growth perspective.   



Still learning every day, but having a repeatable process helped me way more than constantly searching for the “perfect” trade.   



Curious what sectors or setups people here are paying attention to lately.
